_meta.verificationNote: Risk assessment checklist constructed to align with NIST CSF 2.0 functions and subcategories. Items are indicative examples for organizational adaptation and should be tailored to specific assessment scope.

title: CSF 2.0-Aligned Risk Assessment Checklist
description: Checklist of 18 assessment activities organized by NIST CSF 2.0 function. Each item references a specific CSF subcategory and provides verification criteria for risk assessors.

items.title: Organizational Context Documented
items.description: Verify that the organization's mission, applicable legal and regulatory requirements, and stakeholder expectations are documented and available to inform cybersecurity risk decisions.
verificationCriteria: Mission statement references protection of critical services
Legal and regulatory obligation register is current
Stakeholder analysis identifies cybersecurity risk expectations
assessorQuestions: How does your cybersecurity program align with organizational mission?
What regulatory obligations drive your security control requirements?

items.title: Risk Management Strategy Established
items.description: Verify that a cybersecurity risk management strategy exists, is approved by leadership, and defines the approach for identifying, assessing, and treating cyber risks.
verificationCriteria: Risk management strategy document is approved and dated
Strategy covers risk identification, analysis, evaluation, and treatment
Risk appetite and tolerance thresholds are quantified
assessorQuestions: When was your risk management strategy last reviewed and approved?
How do you define the boundary between acceptable and unacceptable risk?

items.title: Supply Chain Risk Management Integrated
items.description: Verify that cybersecurity supply chain risk management processes are established, including vendor risk assessment, contractual requirements, and ongoing monitoring.
verificationCriteria: Vendor risk tiering methodology is documented
Security requirements are included in vendor contracts
Critical vendors undergo periodic security assessment
assessorQuestions: How do you assess the cybersecurity posture of your critical vendors?
What contractual provisions require vendor security incident notification?

items.title: Asset Inventory Complete and Current
items.description: Verify that a comprehensive inventory of hardware, software, data, and services exists and is maintained with defined accuracy and currency standards.
verificationCriteria: Hardware asset inventory matches network discovery scan results
Software inventory includes versions and patch status
Data classification labels are assigned to information assets
Asset owners are designated for all inventoried items
assessorQuestions: How often is your asset inventory reconciled against discovery scans?
What percentage of assets have designated owners?

items.title: Risk Identification Process Active
items.description: Verify that a systematic process exists for identifying cybersecurity risks from internal and external sources, including threat intelligence, vulnerability data, and business change.
verificationCriteria: Threat intelligence sources are documented and actively monitored
Vulnerability scanning covers all in-scope assets
Risk identification considers business process changes and new technologies
Emerging risk identification is a standing agenda item for risk committee
assessorQuestions: What sources do you use to identify new cybersecurity threats?
How do you capture risks arising from business or technology changes?

items.title: Risk Register Maintained
items.description: Verify that a cybersecurity risk register is maintained with inherent and residual risk ratings, treatment plans, risk owners, and review dates for all identified risks.
verificationCriteria: Risk register includes all identified cybersecurity risks
Each risk has inherent and residual ratings using the defined methodology
Treatment plans exist for all risks above appetite threshold
Risk owners are assigned and have acknowledged accountability
assessorQuestions: How many risks are currently in your cyber risk register?
How often are risk ratings reviewed and updated?

items.title: Improvement Process Established
items.description: Verify that lessons learned from incidents, assessments, and exercises are systematically captured and drive improvements to cybersecurity risk management.
verificationCriteria: Post-incident review process is documented and consistently followed
Improvement actions are tracked to completion
Assessment findings are integrated into the risk register
assessorQuestions: How do you capture and track lessons learned from security incidents?
Can you show examples of improvements driven by past assessments?

items.title: Identity and Access Management Controls Verified
items.description: Verify that identity lifecycle management and access control mechanisms are implemented, including provisioning, deprovisioning, privilege management, and multi-factor authentication.
verificationCriteria: User provisioning and deprovisioning processes are documented and followed
Privileged accounts are inventoried and subject to enhanced controls
MFA is enforced for remote access and privileged operations
Access reviews are conducted on a defined schedule
assessorQuestions: What is your average time to deprovision access upon employee termination?
What percentage of privileged accounts have MFA enabled?

items.title: Data Protection Controls Assessed
items.description: Verify that data protection controls including classification, encryption, and loss prevention are implemented for sensitive data at rest and in transit.
verificationCriteria: Data classification policy is defined and applied
Encryption standards are documented for data at rest and in transit
DLP controls are deployed for identified sensitive data categories
Data retention and disposal procedures are documented and followed
assessorQuestions: What encryption standards do you apply to data at rest and in transit?
How do you identify where sensitive data resides across your environment?

items.title: Platform Security Configurations Validated
items.description: Verify that security configurations for platforms, systems, and applications follow documented baselines and are validated through regular assessment.
verificationCriteria: Security configuration baselines are documented per platform type
Configuration compliance is measured against baselines
Deviations are tracked and require approved exceptions
Configuration management process prevents unauthorized changes
assessorQuestions: What configuration baselines do you use (CIS, DISA STIG, vendor)?
What is your current compliance rate against those baselines?

items.title: Continuous Monitoring Operational
items.description: Verify that continuous monitoring capabilities are deployed across networks, systems, and personnel activities, with sufficient coverage of critical assets.
verificationCriteria: SIEM or equivalent collects logs from all critical assets
Network monitoring covers all network segments with sensitive data
Alert rules are defined for known attack patterns
Monitoring coverage gaps are documented and tracked for remediation
assessorQuestions: What percentage of your critical assets are covered by centralized monitoring?
How do you validate that monitoring is working correctly?

items.title: Threat Detection and Analysis Capabilities Assessed
items.description: Verify that the organization has capabilities to detect known and novel threats, correlate events, and escalate confirmed incidents within defined timelines.
verificationCriteria: Detection rules map to threat framework (MITRE ATT&CK or equivalent)
Alert triage process and escalation criteria are documented
Mean time to detect (MTTD) is measured and tracked against targets
Threat intelligence is integrated into detection and analysis workflows
assessorQuestions: What is your current mean time to detect for high-severity incidents?
How do you assess the completeness of your detection rule coverage?

items.title: Incident Response Plan Reviewed
items.description: Verify that a documented incident response plan exists, is regularly tested, and covers incident classification, escalation, containment, eradication, and recovery procedures.
verificationCriteria: IR plan is documented, approved, and version-controlled
Plan defines incident severity levels and escalation criteria
Tabletop exercises are conducted at least annually
Post-exercise improvement actions are tracked to completion
assessorQuestions: When was your incident response plan last tested?
How many tabletop exercises did you conduct in the past 12 months?

items.title: Incident Communication Protocols Verified
items.description: Verify that communication protocols for cybersecurity incidents are predefined, covering internal escalation, regulatory notification, law enforcement coordination, and public disclosure.
verificationCriteria: Communication plan identifies all stakeholders by incident severity
Notification timelines comply with applicable regulatory requirements
Pre-drafted communication templates exist for common scenario types
Media and public communication roles are assigned
assessorQuestions: What is your notification timeline for a data breach affecting customer PII?
Who is authorized to communicate externally about a security incident?

items.title: Forensic Readiness Evaluated
items.description: Verify that the organization maintains digital forensics capabilities, including evidence collection procedures, chain-of-custody documentation, and trained personnel or retainer access.
verificationCriteria: Forensic tools and procedures are documented
Evidence chain-of-custody process is defined
Forensic personnel (internal or retainer) are identified and available
Log retention supports forensic investigation timelines
assessorQuestions: What is your forensic retainer response time SLA?
How do you ensure evidence integrity during an investigation?

items.title: Recovery Plan Existence and Testing
items.description: Verify that recovery plans for cybersecurity incidents are documented, include defined RTO/RPO targets, and are tested regularly through exercises and drills.
verificationCriteria: Recovery plans exist for all critical systems and services
RTO and RPO targets are defined per system tier
Recovery tests demonstrate ability to meet RTO/RPO targets
Test results and identified gaps are documented
assessorQuestions: What are your RTO/RPO targets for tier-1 systems?
When was your last full recovery test, and did it meet targets?

items.title: Recovery Communication Protocols Verified
items.description: Verify that communication protocols for the recovery phase are defined, including status updates to stakeholders, restoration timelines, and service resumption notifications.
verificationCriteria: Recovery communication plan defines update frequency per stakeholder
Restoration status dashboard or reporting mechanism exists
Service resumption criteria and customer notification process are defined
Post-recovery communication includes lessons learned summary
assessorQuestions: How do you communicate recovery progress to affected stakeholders?
What criteria must be met before declaring a service fully restored?

items.title: Cybersecurity Roles and Responsibilities Defined
items.description: Verify that cybersecurity roles, responsibilities, and authorities are formally defined, communicated, and understood across the organization, including accountability for risk decisions.
verificationCriteria: RACI matrix exists for cybersecurity functions
Job descriptions include cybersecurity responsibilities
Risk ownership is formally assigned for all registered risks
Authority to accept risk is documented with approval thresholds
assessorQuestions: Who has authority to accept cybersecurity risk above a defined threshold?
How do you ensure cybersecurity responsibilities are understood by non-IT staff?
